MEDB61EVAMedicalNASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for EVA medical requirements. Documents medical clearance, prebreathe protocol, and health monitoring for extravehicular activities.Questionnairehttps://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/Questionnaire/medb-6-1-eva-medical#0.5.9

Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Sponsor Organizations#medical-operations "Medical Operations"
overview.disciplineDiscipline1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Medical Disciplines#eva-medical "EVA Medical"
overview.purposePurpose/Objectives1..1textInitial Value: string = To ensure crew medical fitness for EVA, document prebreathe protocol compliance, and monitor crew health during and after EVA operations.

Initial Value: Coding = NASA Medical Data Archive Systems#mmis "Medical Mission Information System"

Documentation for this format

Options Sets

Answer options for overview.sponsor

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-sponsor-cs#medical-operations ("Medical Operations")

Answer options for overview.discipline

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-discipline-cs#eva-medical ("EVA Medical")

Answer options for pre-eva-activities.prebreathe-protocol

  • Campout (overnight in airlock)
  • In-suit Light Exercise (ISLE)
  • Exercise Prebreathe Protocol

Answer options for post-eva-assessment.general-condition

  • Good - no issues
  • Fatigued but well
  • Minor discomfort
  • Requires medical attention

Answer options for data-delivery.archive

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-data-archive-cs#mmis ("Medical Mission Information System")

extension.valueString: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

name: MEDB61EVAMedical
title: MEDB 6.1 EVA Medical Requirements
description: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for EVA medical requirements. Documents medical clearance, prebreathe protocol, and health monitoring for extravehicular activities.
purpose: To document medical requirements for EVA including crew certification, prebreathe protocol compliance, and post-EVA health assessment.
copyright: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

initial.valueString: To ensure crew medical fitness for EVA, document prebreathe protocol compliance, and monitor crew health during and after EVA operations.
Medical clearance, prebreathe compliance, vitals during EVA, DCS symptoms, post-EVA assessment
EVA medical training including DCS recognition, emergency procedures, and suit operations from medical perspective.
